rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative radical selenylation/1,2-carbon migration of alkenylcyclobutanols with diselenides to give beta-selenyl cyclopentanones, which undergo Dowd-Beckwith ring expansion to cyclohexanones.
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of diaryl diselenide to a diselenide radical cation that fragments to a selenyl radical (and selenyl cation); a further anodic oxidation converts the carbon-radical adduct to a carbocation that undergoes semipinacol 1,2-alkyl migration. Cathode reduces protons to H2.

key_intermediates.name: carbon-centered radical from PhSe addition to alkenylcyclobutanol (IV)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh to a radical cation that cleaves to PhSe• and PhSe+; PhSe• adds to the vinyl group of the alkenylcyclobutanol forming a benzylic C-radical, which is further oxidized at the anode to a carbocation; 1,2-alkyl migration (semipinacol ring expansion of the cyclobutanol) gives the beta-selenyl cyclopentanone, while the cathode reduces H+ to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Product formation suppressed to trace, supporting a radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 62%, consistent with partial radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethene
probe_reagents.observation: Yield reduced to 57%, consistent with a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: no current control
probe_reagents.observation: No reaction without applied current, confirming electrochemical initiation

notes: Authors propose two competing pathways: favored path a involves PhSe• radical addition to the alkene followed by anodic oxidation to a cation and semipinacol 1,2-migration; alternative path b invokes nucleophilic attack of the alkenylcyclobutanol on PhSe+ (cation III) followed by 1,2-carbon migration, which could not be ruled out. CV showed oxidation peaks of 1a at 1.74 V and PhSeSePh (2a) at 1.33 V, consistent with diselenide being oxidized first. A subsequent radical-initiated Dowd-Beckwith ring expansion (Bu3SnH/AIBN) converts the beta-selenyl cyclopentanones to cyclohexanones.
